text:
My son was born last month and I just opened his first custodial account on $HOOD.

And guess what his first asset was?

Bitcoin.

$NVDA says Blackwell software optimizations improved DeepSeek V4 performance by ~5x in one month cutting token costs to ~20% of prior levels.

Nvidia’s value keeps compounding after deployment as software, CUDA, networking and hardware drive more throughput from the same GPUs.

$AAPL is adding $AXP “Pay with Points” to Apple Pay letting eligible Amex cardholders redeem Membership Rewards points directly at checkout. 

Users can apply points to all or part of a purchase when paying with an eligible Amex card through Apple Pay on iPhone or iPad.

$AMBA is my largest position in the “Physical AI” basket within my growth portfolio: 

savvytrader.com/StockSavvySh…

$AMBA up over 22% as the market wakes up to the importance of the edge AI silicon layer behind robots, drones, autonomous systems and intelligent cameras.

Physical AI needs machines that can see, interpret and act locally in real time and Ambarella builds the chips and software platform that help make that possible.

$AMZN AWS is investing $1B to embed AI engineers directly with customers to help them build and deploy AI applications.

The move expands AWS’s forward-deployed engineering model putting AI specialists alongside customer teams to accelerate real enterprise adoption.
$OUST is now up over 250% in the past 3 months 😳

$BB is integrating AtHoc with $MSFT Teams and Entra ID to bring crisis alerts and identity management directly into enterprise workflows.

The update strengthens BlackBerry’s emergency coordination platform with real-time comments, ArcGIS mapping and improved dispatch tools.

$NVDA is expanding its China robotics team by hiring for 12+ roles across embodied intelligence, simulation and robotics implementation.

The push supports Nvidia’s GR00T, Cosmos and GPU robotics platforms reinforcing physical AI as one of its next major growth layers.

$AMZN plans to open a fully robotic logistics center in Poland in October 2026.

The facility is expected to create more than 1,000 jobs in its first year expanding Amazon’s automation footprint in Europe.
SK Hynix will be using the ticker $SKHY

SK Hynix has reportedly filed for a U.S. listing on Nasdaq as it looks to raise ~$30B.

The funding would support new South Korean chip fabs and expand AI memory production as HBM demand continues to surge.

$MU just printed software-like margins in DRAM and HBM showing how take-or-pay contracts and HBM scarcity are changing the pricing dynamic.

The clearest AI signal was Core Data Center revenue growing 653% driven by HBM and high-capacity DRAM for AI servers which makes Micron more of a toll collector on AI infrastructure with demand broadening across cloud, data center, mobile and auto.

$OUST said its Rev8 OS digital lidar sensors now meet Build America, Buy America requirements making them eligible for federally funded infrastructure projects.

Ouster is positioning its lidar + AI traffic platform, BlueCity directly into U.S.-funded smart infrastructure.

Two months ago, Worth Charting Options Income ETF ($WRTH) opened for trading.  

We thought you'd appreciate some "facts & figures" relating to the Fund.

While 2 months of performance is immaterial/irrelevant/altogether meaningless… we are pleased how things are going, and especially pleased that WRTH has done what it is supposed to do, has done what it is built to do, since we launched on Tuesday April 28th.

The table below is informative.

It details WRTH’s risk adjusted performance, and WRTH’s performance relative to other, income oriented and low-volatility ETFs.

XYLD (1.723 Sharpe) and WRTH (1.645 Sharpe) lead the group with WRTH posting the lowest max drawdown (-0.8%) and lowest volatility (3.6%).

I think the ~10 minutes from this time stamp of @biancoresearch is an accurate portrayal of the long developing political situation and it's potential (if not likely) outcome on the economic situation.

This has been a growing concern of mine since ~2016:
piped.video/vYUrgZCdNm4?t=1546

7/7

The next chart is the same but begins on June 2nd, the last all-time high in the S&P 500. It is down 3.36% in three weeks (black).

Normally, a stock market falling about 1% a week would be concerning. But see the orange line, the 459 non-AI stocks. They are up 2.76% over the same period, a far different read on the economy. The decline was driven by the 41 AI stocks (red) falling 12.67% in the last three weeks.
----
The U.S. stock market might be two asset classes and should be treated as such. This is neither good nor bad, but it needs to be understood, and investment outlooks and interpretations need to be adjusted accordingly.

6/7

This distinction of two asset classes matters for two reasons: index investing and market signaling. Index investing is somewhat obvious, as nearly half of an index’s holdings are now tied to AI stocks. But what about market signaling? What is the stock market telling us about the economy?

Consider the following two charts that show the same thing, differing only by time. The first starts on February 28, the day the Iran War began. The black line shows the S&P 500 is up almost 7% in four months, an impressive run. A standard interpretation is that the economy is doing well. But see the orange line, the 459 non-AI stocks. They are only up 1.5%, with the positive gains coming in the last few days. That suggests a very different interpretation of the economy.

Nearly the entire 7% gain on the S&P 500 since February 28 was driven by AI stocks (red).

5/7

And this divergence between AI and non-AI stocks is showing up in the record divergence of other indices, such as the one below, which compares the S&P 500 market-cap-weighted index to its equal-weighted counterpart.

4/7

And AI and non-AI stocks are starting to diverge from each other, as the following chart shows.

3/7

In his May 22 “Flow Show,” Michael Hartnett of BofA highlighted the chart below. It shows the stock market is the most concentrated in a single theme in 150 years. The last time we saw anything resembling this level of concentration was the railroads in the late 19th century.

(Note: To say the stock market is the most concentrated in 150 years, we are using the table above showing a 49% peak, not the “AI Big 10” showing 40% that Hartnett uses below.)

2/7

They account for 44% of the S&P 500’s market cap and hit a peak of 49% earlier this month.

1/7

Is the Stock Market Two Asset Classes?

Updated 🧵

We have been arguing that the stock market is effectively two asset classes: AI stocks and non-AI stocks. There are 41 AI-related stocks in the S&P 500.

Maybe the problem is that we compare this to investing.

How leveraged ETFs compare to prediction markets and sports betting might be a better comparison.
2 Hours before the NYSE Open

On Friday (repost), I noted the US announced it attacked Iran 36 minutes after the NYSE closed.

Well:

23 minutes after the Asian markets opened this evening,

The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce.

📺 DON'T TRUST THIS GAP UP JUST YET + $MU HEALTHY CONSOLIDATION? + $SPCX NEAR A BREAKOUT?

Get today's Micron & SanDisk Update: t3live.com/rd

I break down why traders should be careful chasing today's strong gap higher after last week's sharp pullback. 

While the market is short-term oversold and due for a bounce, the real test is whether the $QQQ can reclaim and hold above key moving averages or if this becomes another failed rally. 

Rather than assuming the correction is over, you should stay tactical, trim into strength, and wait for confirmation before adding significant exposure.

*

$QQQ sits near an important technical decision point. 

You should closely watch whether it can hold above roughly the $715 area and reclaim nearby moving averages. 

If buyers fail to maintain the opening strength, another rejection could keep the recent pullback intact. 

Don't chase the opening gap today.

*

The semiconductor sector $SOXX $SMH remains the market's biggest focus. 

I think $MU may now be entering a healthy consolidation phase following its massive rally. 

Instead of chasing the stock higher or aggressively shorting it, let it digest gains and rebuild its technical structure. 

$SNDK recent pullback simply brought it back toward its short-term moving average rather than signaling the end of its trend.

*

We captured a significant options gain in $SPCX after buying calls during Friday's pullback. 

With rumors of a Nasdaq-100 inclusion approaching, the stock needs to hold the $147-$150 area and ultimately reclaim $158-$160 to build enough momentum for another breakout.

*

So, don't assume one strong opening erases last week's technical damage. 

The market is attempting a relief rally, but confirmation matters. 

*

5 defensive sectors at |Z|>2σ in the same week.

XLV +4.6σ. XLRE +3.4σ. XLU +2.4σ. XLP +2.3σ. LQD +2.1σ.

Tech printed -2σ on the other side. Credit confirmed risk-off.

The largest cross-asset signal the framework has produced in over a year.

The market is at new all time highs BECAUSE of the carry trade and Yen.

Don’t you all see it?

Do you see how the Yen is behaving?

Suspected intervention and complete abject failure.

They must panic.

My thesis is here.

Prior to the 1987 stock market crash, the Dow Jones Industrial Average was up around 30% for the year.

Erased in 1 day.

They will call me a guru.

Like they did in 2011 during the Summer Crash.

Like they did in 2018 during the Christmas Massacre.

Like they did during the Covid crash.

Like they did in the subsequent melt-up after.

They will do it again because of Japan.

There are no gurus.

Only cycles.

My time has finally arrived.

2 years wrong.

10 years right.

I did not relent.

I won’t.

I am not a perma-bear.

I simply saw the inevitable before anyone else did.

Phase 2 of the carry trade is at our doorstep.

The Main Event is closer than ever.

And you are not ready.

This time, I am.

Goodnight.
